msg: warning: Variable xGridSpacing is a dead store!
reasoning: Line 24 is 'int xGridSpacing = left_probe_bed_position;' inside #ifdef AUTO_BED_LEVELING_GRID (which is itself always defined when ENABLE_AUTO_BED_LEVELING is defined, via line 2's #define). xGridSpacing is assigned here but never read again — the function has no further code that uses it. Genuine dead store. Presence condition def(ENABLE_AUTO_BED_LEVELING) is correctly constrained (AUTO_BED_LEVELING_GRID is a consequence of ENABLE_AUTO_BED_LEVELING, so the combined guard collapses to the single condition). The vbdb entry for e30bfedce4d marks '// ERROR' at line 21 (left_probe_bed_position using LEFT_PROBE_BED_POSITION, which is only defined under AUTO_BED_LEVELING_GRID — a different uninitialized/undefined macro issue). The dead store of xGridSpacing is a new finding. New true positive.
